steps.title: Spot the pivotal claim
steps.content:
The passage opens with Nixon's Watergate scandal, then pivots sharply with “But Nixon's legacy is complex:” — signaling that the text will balance negative and positive.

This claim sets up the passage's structure: acknowledge the scandal, then present counterbalancing achievements (EPA, healthcare reform).

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A assesses Nixon's significance by considering both achievements (EPA, healthcare) and failings (Watergate scandal), which is exactly what the text does through its balanced structure.

The other three either introduce a time-passage claim unsupported by the passage (B), wrongly attribute his prominence to associates (C), or ignore the scandal to focus only on achievements (D).

That matches choice A — A is correct.
